summary: McKenzie briefly announces a detailed article he wrote about integrating Mixpanel analytics with Ruby on Rails. He highlights Mixpanel's funnel tracking as particularly valuable despite being more technically demanding than most micro-ISV owners might prefer. He notes that the analytics insights have already led to changes in Bingo Card Creator, and mentions he's on an optimization kick before the busy school year season.
key_points: Mixpanel's funnel tracking capability is exceptionally valuable for understanding user conversion paths
There's a tradeoff between analytics power and technical complexity that matters for solo software businesses
Seasonal business cycles (school year) create urgency to optimize before peak demand
money_quotes: It is probably slightly more technically involved than most uISVs would care for, but the funnel tracking is solid gold.
Got to get ready for the school year! Three more weeks or so and I won't be able to break the website NEARLY so often.
key_insight: Funnel tracking tools like Mixpanel provide outsized value for conversion optimization even when they require more technical setup than typical small-business analytics.
